contextBanner: [TBG | Continuum Interoperability | harness architecture | branch: feat/continuum-harness-interoperability]
purpose: Expose BlacksmithGuild harness capabilities to Continuum through a versioned, one-way packet while preserving BlacksmithGuild as a standalone app and authority over its runtime, proof, and domain adapters.

producer.authority: BlacksmithGuild owns capability classification, app adapters, proof boundaries, and removal decisions.

allowedScope: inventorying cross-cutting harness capabilities already proven useful in BlacksmithGuild
exporting capability metadata, source paths, ownership boundaries, and migration readiness
letting Continuum consume generic contracts or reimplement generic orchestration behind an optional adapter
validating that BlacksmithGuild remains buildable, testable, and operable without Continuum
preserving app-specific policy, workflow names, evidence semantics, and game/runtime adapters in BlacksmithGuild
forbiddenScope: making Continuum a BlacksmithGuild build, validation, launch, or runtime dependency
moving route, smithing, economy, trade, campaign, save, launcher, or gameplay decisions into a generic Continuum core
deleting BlacksmithGuild harness code before a replacement has parity evidence and an app-owned adapter
allowing Continuum to redefine BlacksmithGuild proof levels or claim installed-game behavior
cross-repository mutation from the export command
claiming runtime proof from this static interoperability contract

classifications.meaning: Cross-cutting capability whose generic core may be implemented in Continuum after parity tests exist.
Repository-owned policy or adapter that may call a generic Continuum capability but remains authoritative in BlacksmithGuild.
Game, launcher, save, campaign, route, smithing, trade, economy, or runtime behavior that must remain outside Continuum's generic core.

capabilities.summary: Inspect exact-head checks, review state, mergeability, holds, reconciliation, and bounded squash merge decisions.
genericCore: required, conditional-required, and advisory check classification
draft promotion and deterministic merge eligibility
blacksmithAdapter: installed-game and OS-specific validation remains advisory

capabilities.summary: Map branches, PRs, worktrees, conflicts, dirty state, stale value, and safe sprint bases before implementation.
Classify proposed logic as cross-cutting harness, narrow skill/domain behavior, or rejected ceremony.
Coordinate concurrent branches and worktrees through replay, validation, evidence retention, first-user checks, and clean closeout.
Render policy and decision state as separate machine-readable and syntactic-English reports.
Keep installed-game, launcher, save, campaign, movement, trade, smithing, and live behavior authority inside BlacksmithGuild.
blacksmithAdapter: movement, route, trade, economy, and smithing behavior
installed-game evidence and live runtime proof

migrationRules: Require a Continuum-side parity test for every generic capability before BlacksmithGuild delegates to it.
Keep a BlacksmithGuild adapter that owns repo policy, paths, workflow names, proof vocabulary, and domain boundaries.
Run BlacksmithGuild validation with Continuum unavailable before accepting any delegation.
Remove duplicated BlacksmithGuild generic code only in a later explicit extraction sprint with rollback instructions.
Treat installed-game Windows and Linux runs as advisory evidence unless a narrowly scoped app-runtime sprint explicitly requires one.

orderedSteps.name: inventory
requiredEvidence: generic core and app adapter split

orderedSteps.name: extract_deliberately
requiredEvidence: no loss of BlacksmithGuild policy or runtime authority

requires: generic candidate cores exclude app-domain behavior
